title: How students are using AI in 2026: A shift from AI adoption to AI agency
summary: Analysis of how students are shifting from passive AI adoption to active AI agency in 2026

reporting_brief.WHO: Higher education institutions, student body, educators facing AI integration challenge
reporting_brief.WHAT: Student AI usage surged to 92% (2026) vs 66% (2024); but training gap persists - only 36% received formal institutional support; socioeconomic divide in AI application
reporting_brief.WHY: Critical shift from asking 'Are students using AI?' to 'Is AI creating dependency or agency?'; risk of over-automation without learning depth
reporting_brief.CONTEXT: Post 'panic phase' (2023) and 'pilot phase' (2024); AI now foundational infrastructure; 53% of students fear academic integrity accusations despite legitimate use

PERSPECTIVES.education: Need for structured AI literacy and pedagogical guardrails; risk of outsourcing thinking
PERSPECTIVES.student: Anxiety over academic integrity; uncertainty about boundaries of responsible use
PERSPECTIVES.research: Socioeconomic disparity in AI application quality - high-SES students use for deep research, others for surface tasks

reporting_brief.SUGGESTED_ANGLE: The AI paradox: Student adoption hits 92%, but institutions still haven't solved the training gap
